qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Upload destination parameter allows writes into runtime directories
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about `/api/upload` path rewriting and arbitrary runtime file writes in `server/api/projects/index.js`; the advisory is a missing guest authorization check on `POST/DELETE /api/scheduler` while `secureEnabled=true`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default unauthenticated mode grants admin API and socket access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding relies on `secureEnabled=false` causing a global auth bypass and admin group `-1`; the advisory instead fixes guest-token access to scheduler mutations even when `secureEnabled=true`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable scheduler guest-auth bypass or the associated heartbeat-to-guest-token path. The qualifying findings instead cover an `/api/upload` arbitrary write, a separate `secureEnabled=false` global admin bypass, and an unrelated import deserialization issue.
evidence: The upstream advisory patch adds `isGuestUser()` in `server/api/jwt-helper.js` and blocks guest users only in `server/api/scheduler/index.js` for `POST /api/scheduler` and `DELETE /api/scheduler`, showing the root cause is missing guest authorization on scheduler mutations.

The same patch also adjusts `POST /api/heartbeat` in `server/api/index.js`, which is where guest-token behavior relevant to the advisory lives; none of the session findings references that heartbeat flow.

The `default-unauthenticated-admin-access` finding depends on `secureEnabled=false` and `verifyGroups()` returning admin `-1`, while the advisory explicitly impacts deployments with `secureEnabled=true` and does not rely on the global auth-disabled mode.

The `api-upload-runtime-dir-write` finding targets `/api/upload` writes in `server/api/projects/index.js`, and the `example` finding targets a non-scheduler import deserialization path, so neither matches the advisory-described vulnerable code.
